AR layer for guided tours

Relic Quest

A treasure hunt hidden inside the tour you already take — scan artifacts, crack open crystals, and walk away with a relic worth sharing. Built for delight, made to spread.

Dex is Headout's AI audio-guide app — cinematic, location-aware tours live in New York, London, Paris, Rome, Florence and more. Relic Quest is an AR layer on top of it.

Built on the Dex tour · The Relics
The problem

Tours are one-dimensional.

Guests leak out at two clear points: half never start a tour after opening the app, and a third of those who do start quit mid-tour. End to end, only about a third finish.

50%bounce before starting a tour
30%more drop off mid-tour
35%complete, app open to finish
What gamification solves

Each leak has a mechanic that fixes it.

50%

Pre-start gap

A relic-hunt teaser on the pre-tour screen — "four fragments hidden across the Tower, can you find them all?" — gives a reason to begin before checkpoint one.

30%

Mid-tour dead time

The walk between stops becomes the game. You're not walking to the next checkpoint, you're hunting the next fragment.

5.5×

They want more

Avg 5.5 bot queries per active session. Talking to a historical character isn't a bolt-on — it's what engaged guests already reach for.

From flat to dimensional

Dex made tours 2D. We add the third dimension.

1D

The old tour

Passive. Go through the motions, snap a photo, leave. Nothing earned, nothing kept.

2D

Dex changed it

An AI-powered audio guide made tours responsive — ask anything, go deeper, at your pace.

3D

Relic Quest

Now it's interactive. A hunt that leaves you with a souvenir and memories that stick.

The idea

A collectible AR hunt, layered onto the tour you already take.

  • Rides a shipping product — Dex is already live and reviewed daily across landmark tours; no new behavior to teach.
  • Dead time becomes the game — the walk between stops is where the magic hides.
How it works · 01 · Find

"There's something hidden nearby."

When you pass a hidden artifact, the app nudges you. Raise your phone, scan the room, and find what's been disguised in plain sight.

How it works · 02 · Come alive

The artifact comes to life.

Scan the right target and it animates in AR — a still wall piece springs into motion and colour, like it woke up. Hold it a moment and a relic drops.

How it works · 03 · Crack it open

Tap to crack the crystal.

Each relic arrives sealed in a crystal. Tap and it charges with light, fractures, breaks through, and the shards burst away — revealing the 3D relic piece inside. A five-beat ritual that makes every find feel earned.

The hook

One piece of seven.

Every relic is a fragment of a larger badge. Six are hidden across the tour; the seventh, completing piece only appears once you've found all six. Miss one and the badge stays open.

The payoff

The badge completes.

  • A real souvenir, tied to a specific place and day.
  • Every piece is interactive 3D — rotate it, look closely, and find hidden easter eggs.
  • The emotional close a normal tour never gives you.
Built to spread

Every finish is worth sharing.

The tour ends on a card people actually want to post — duration, artifacts scanned, questions asked, plus surprising moments like "you asked Anne Boleyn about her final words; only 12% did." The relic and the stats share as one image, turning every visitor into a tiny ad for the next one.

It compounds

A shelf of every journey.

Relics live on your profile — Headout, the Louvre, the Colosseum — each a complete badge from a real tour. Tap any one to revisit its stats and share again. A growing trophy case that pulls people back.

Why this isn't a guess

We're not betting on new behavior.

Dex tours are already live and growing. Relic Quest attaches to a habit people have today — the risk isn't whether anyone shows up, it's how much deeper we can pull them in.

The demand is already here

A growing audience — with room to finish stronger.

~296
Weekly active users
up from ~240 five weeks ago
35%
Complete a tour, app open to finish
the headroom Relic Quest goes after
↑
Built to lift
tour completion
↑
Built to spark
shares & word of mouth

The audience is here and growing week over week — only about a third of app-openers finish a tour today, and engaged users go deep (avg 5.5 bot queries a session). Relic Quest's job is to make the experience delightful enough to finish and shareable enough to spread.

The opportunity: AR in travel & tourism was ~$20B in 2023 and growing fast; ~1.46B active iPhones, every Dex-capable device already AR-capable — zero added hardware barrier. Dex's own roadmap already names games.

What we shipped

A complete loop, built end to end in 42 hours.

Find

AR scan

Hidden artifacts detected between checkpoints.

Reveal

Video-on-target

The artifact animates, then drops a crystal.

Open

Crack-to-3D

Tap-to-shatter reveal of the relic piece.

Keep

Badge + profile

Seven-piece assembly, stats, share, shelf.

What's next

The hunt is just the first map.

Now
Working demo

Full loop running on a real tour, end to end.

Now
Automated authoring

Relic creation and adding the hunt to a Dex tour already run on tooling we've built.

Next
Venue pilot

Measure completion and share lift at one live site.

Later
Live artifacts

AR-powered selfies & photo album; conversational AR characters; seasonal relics.

The mechanic is venue-agnostic, and the pipeline to build and ship a hunt is largely automated. Any guided tour with a phone and a wall becomes a hunt.

Relic Quest

Don't just visit.
Walk away with it.

We gamified the guided tour — a passive walkthrough becomes an interactive hunt that ends in a souvenir, a story, and a reason to come back.
